questions.question:
To successfully cultivate a thriving vegetable garden, ________ that the soil is properly aerated and the moisture levels remain consistent throughout the growing season.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: it is necessary for a gardener to ensure
ensuring is necessary for a gardener
the soil must be ensured by a gardener
questions.explanation: Choice C correctly places 'a gardener' immediately after the introductory infinitive phrase, ensuring the modifier logically attaches to the actor performing the cultivation. Choice A uses an expletive subject that severs the modifier link, Choice B is unidiomatic, and Choice D incorrectly makes 'the soil' the logical actor.

questions.question:
The curator argued that the collection was noteworthy for its chronological breadth, its thematic diversity, and ________.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice C correctly maintains list parallelism by matching the established pattern [possessive pronoun + noun + prepositional phrase]. Choices A, B, and D shift to clause, gerund, and infinitive structures, respectively, breaking the grammatical symmetry.

questions.question:
Though commonly associated with the desert, ________ to a wide variety of environments, including coastal scrub and high-altitude grasslands.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: it is the coyote that has adapted
questions.explanation: Choice A correctly places 'the coyote' as the subject immediately following the introductory modifier, ensuring the phrase logically describes the organism. Choices B and D incorrectly attach the modifier to 'adaptation' (a process, not a desert-dweller), and Choice C is unnecessarily wordy.

questions.question:
The recent study on urban heat islands found that planting more trees is not only effective at lowering surface temperatures ________ air quality in densely populated neighborhoods.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly maintains prepositional parallelism within the 'not only... but also' structure by repeating 'at' ('effective at lowering... but also at improving'). Choice A inserts an unnecessary pronoun/verb, Choice C drops the required preposition, and Choice D changes 'at' to 'in,' breaking the parallel pattern.

questions.question:
After meticulously reviewing the architectural blueprints for the new library, ________ that the ventilation system required significant modifications to meet safety codes.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: it was discovered by the head contractor
the discovery was made by the head contractor
there was a discovery by the head contractor
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly identifies 'the head contractor' as the subject of the main clause, ensuring the introductory participial phrase logically attaches to the person performing the review. Choices A, C, and D create dangling modifiers by placing inanimate or existential subjects in the actor position.

questions.question:
The literary critic argued that the imagery in the newcomer's poetry was more evocative than ________, despite the latter's much larger readership.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly uses the singular demonstrative pronoun 'that' to logically compare 'imagery' to equivalent imagery. Choice A incorrectly compares imagery to a person, Choice C creates an unparallel clause, and Choice D uses the plural 'those,' which disagrees with the singular 'imagery.'

questions.question:
Known for his ability to blend traditional jazz with avant-garde influences, ________ was celebrated by the global music community for its sheer technical brilliance.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: the latest performance by the saxophonist
the saxophonist delivered a performance that
it was the saxophonist's latest performance that
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly links the introductory modifier to 'the saxophonist' via the possessive construction, which is standard when the performance itself is the subject of the main clause. Choice A places the performance first, weakening the modifier link. Choice C disrupts the sentence flow, and Choice D uses an unnecessary cleft construction.

questions.question:
The software update was intended to improve the user interface, strengthen data security, and ________ overall system latency.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice D correctly uses the base verb 'reduce' to maintain parallelism with the distributed infinitive structure '[to] improve, [to] strengthen, and [to] reduce.' Choices A and B are noun phrases, and Choice C is a gerund, all of which break the verb-list symmetry.

questions.question:
By utilizing a specialized drone equipped with LIDAR technology, ________ to map the hidden ruins of the Mayan city through the dense forest canopy.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
options: it was possible for the archaeologists
there was a way for the archaeologists
questions.explanation: Choice B correctly places 'the archaeologists' as the subject immediately following the introductory phrase, ensuring the modifier logically attaches to the users of the drone. Choices A and D use filler subjects ('it' and 'there'), and Choice C illogically suggests the ruins utilized the drone.

questions.question:
The restaurant's signature dish is not only famous for its complex blend of spices ________ its elaborate presentation.

Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
questions.explanation: Choice D correctly maintains parallelism within the 'not only... but also' structure by repeating the preposition 'for' ('famous for X but also for Y'). Choices A, B, and C either omit the required preposition or use incorrect conjunction pairings that break the idiomatic and grammatical symmetry.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately identify modifier placement, parallel structure, and logical comparison rules to maintain grammatical precision.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether introductory modifiers attach to the correct subject and list items share identical grammatical forms.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: 'not only' requires 'but also,' comparisons must match logically, and modifiers must attach to the actor immediately following the comma.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: Who is performing the opening action? Are the compared items equivalent? Do parallel list items share the same grammatical structure?
